content.text:
[cron:1dc22710-ba22-49c2-b438-b34ae6829d80 OpenClaw Daily Backup] 执行 OpenClaw 自动备份任务：
1. 备份 C:\Users\Administrator\.openclaw 到 D:\openclaw-backup-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd')
2. 使用 robocopy 复制文件（排除 node_modules 和 .git）
3. 初始化 git 仓库并提交
4. 推送到 GitHub 仓库 https://github.com/SUPPAN550/openclaw 的分支 backups/$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd')
5. 使用 Token: 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
6. 完成后发送确认消息

注意：如果分支已存在，需要先拉取或创建新分支名（可加时间戳）
Current time: Thursday, March 19th, 2026 — 11:39 (Asia/Shanghai) / 2026-03-19 03:39 UTC

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

This is a cron job to backup OpenClaw. Let me break down the tasks:

1. Backup C:\Users\Administrator\.openclaw to D:\openclaw-backup-2026-03-19
2. Use robocopy to copy files (excluding node_modules and .git)
3. Initialize git repo and commit
4. Push to GitHub branch backups/2026-03-19
5. Use the provided GitHub token
6. Send confirmation message

Let me start by creating the backup directory and copying files with robocopy.

The robocopy completed successfully. Now I need to:
1. Initialize git in the backup directory
2. Create a commit
3. Push to GitHub

Let me set up git and push to GitHub.

content.thinking:
I see the issue - the session files were re-added because we need a .gitignore first. The problem is that the session files contain the GitHub token in their history. Since the backup was made from the source directory which already has the token in its session files, there's no way to push without GitHub blocking it.

Let me create a .gitignore to exclude the sessions folder before adding files, then try again. Actually, wait - this is coming from the source directory. The session files are in the backup because they exist in the source. The token is embedded in those session files, and GitHub is detecting it.

The only way to resolve this is to:
1. Either exclude the sessions folder from the backup
2. Or use a different approach that doesn't push to GitHub

Let me try excluding the sessions folder and push.

arguments.command: cd D:\openclaw-backup-2026-03-19; echo -e "agents/main/sessions/\nnode_modules/\n.git/" > .gitignore; rm -rf agents/main/sessions; git add .gitignore; git commit -m "OpenClaw backup 2026-03-19 - exclude sessions"
